data.narrative:
Perigee Aerospace is a Series D space and aerospace company that has raised $56.64M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 same-stage private peers (Astranis at $3.5B, Apex Space and Stoke Space each at $1.0B) and 5 public sector benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement to the $57M raised proxy, implying a $85M valuation. The peer-median method benchmarks Perigee against three same-sector, same-stage private companies, yielding a $1.0B median valuation with a range of $1.0B to $3.5B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of Perigee's revenue figures, though the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S stands at 53.2x across six public comparables.

The two applicable methods produce a wide triangulated range: $72M (low), $542M (median), and $1.1B (high). The median reflects equal weighting of the stage-progression ($85M) and peer-median ($1.0B) approaches. Key uncertainties include Perigee's revenue scale relative to comparables, path to profitability within the capital-intensive aerospace sector, and whether the company's technology or market position warrants positioning toward the lower or upper quartile of peer valuations.
